reasoning: Use -re/-ra or -be/-ba for ‘to’; vowel harmony follows the last vowel (front→e, back→a). Final t behaves like Budapest→Budapestre, so with front vowel é we get -re: Kecskemétre.
Ends in -n like Sopron→Sopronba; last vowel o is back → -ba.
Ends in -n, and the last vowel e is front → -be.
Final vowel ű is front; pattern like Elyüs→Elyüsre → use -re.
Foreign name; last vowel i is front → -be (cf. Miami→Miamibe).
Final a is back → -ba, and word‑final a lengthens before the suffix (cf. Ottawa→Ottawába).
Hungarian name ending in -s; last vowel e is front → -re (cf. Elyüs→Elyüsre).
Foreign name; last vowel a is back → -ba.
Ends in -n like Sopron→Sopronba; last vowel o is back → -ba.
Names ending in -falva take -ra/-re; last vowel a is back → -ra, with a→á lengthening.
Final a is back → -ba; final a lengthens before the suffix.
Names in -háza take -ra/-re; last vowel a is back → -ra, with a→á lengthening.
Final vowel i is front → -be (cf. Ploiesti→Ploiestibe).
As in the table earlier: -ra with a→á for names ending in -háza.
Ends with -d like Szeged→Szegedre/Nagyszombat→Nagyszombatra; last vowel á is back → -ra, giving -dra.
